applications-drug-discovery.claim_text: Multimodal text-genomic models accelerate drug target identification, RNA-therapeutic design, and clinical hypothesis generation by linking the literature corpus to candidate biological sequences in a single generative system.

candidates.paper_title: GeneCompass: deciphering universal gene regulatory mechanisms with a knowledge-informed cross-species foundation model

useful_outcomes.rationale: Cross-species single-cell foundation model improves multiple regulatory/drug-relevant tasks and identifies factors that drive cell-fate transitions, including a stem-cell differentiation experiment.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained foundation model supports fine-tuning for target identification and perturbation tasks.

plausible.rationale: Validated through both retrospective benchmarks and a wet-lab differentiation experiment.

scalable.rationale: Trained on >100M single cells; supports genome-scale and pan-cell-type analysis.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple downstream task benchmarks plus an experimental cell-fate perturbation.

candidates.paper_title: Signals in the Cells: Multimodal and Contextualized Machine Learning Foundations for Therapeutics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Releases datasets, models, and benchmarks for drug-target nomination, perturbation response, and protein-peptide interaction with cell-type context.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Therapeutics Commons (TDC-2) is directly consumable: datasets, foundation-model tooling, and benchmarks for drug discovery.

plausible.rationale: Built on established TDC infrastructure; well-scoped task definitions.

scalable.rationale: Designed for large-scale ML benchmarking across many therapeutic tasks.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides four explicit benchmark tasks with held-out evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Accurate RNA 3D structure prediction using a language model-based deep learning approach

useful_outcomes.rationale: RNA-LM-based 3D structure predictor outperforms single-modality baselines and is positioned for RNA-targeting drug discovery.

immediate_benefit.rationale: End-to-end pipeline available for RNA-targeted therapeutic structural modeling.

plausible.rationale: Validated on RNA-Puzzles and CASP15 with cross-family benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Inference scales to many single-chain RNAs; not yet at full transcriptome RNP scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple held-out RNA structure benchmarks, time-censored evaluation, secondary-structure cross-checks.

candidates.paper_title: GenerRNA: A generative pre-trained language model for de novo RNA design

useful_outcomes.rationale: Generative RNA LM produces de novo sequences with stable secondary structure and demonstrated high target-protein affinity in fine-tuned settings.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open checkpoints and fine-tuning workflow directly usable for RNA-therapeutic design.

plausible.rationale: GPT-style RNA generation with structural and binding follow-ups; sample sizes modest.

scalable.rationale: Generative inference scales to large candidate libraries.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via secondary-structure stability metrics and protein-affinity assays.

candidates.paper_title: BioNeMo Framework: a modular, high-performance library for AI model development in drug discovery

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates training large protein/biology LMs at scale (3B-param BERT pLM in 4.2 days on 256 A100s); enables but does not itself deliver multimodal drug-discovery results.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source modular framework directly usable to build drug-discovery foundation models.

plausible.rationale: Well-engineered framework with concrete pretraining benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Explicitly designed for hundreds-of-GPU training, demonstrating clinical/industrial scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Mainly engineering benchmarks; downstream validation deferred to user models.

candidates.paper_title: GeneCompass: Deciphering Universal Gene Regulatory Mechanisms with Knowledge-Informed Cross-Species Foundation Model

useful_outcomes.rationale: Same line of work as MMG-069; preprint version with similar drug-target nomination and regulatory benchmarks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Preprint version; superseded by Cell Research release for production use.

plausible.rationale: Methodology validated in benchmarks but final venue version is more rigorous.

scalable.rationale: >100M single-cell pretraining demonstrates scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Downstream benchmarks reported; validation strategy parallels journal version.

candidates.paper_title: Artificial intelligence-driven circRNA vaccine development: multimodal collaborative optimization and a new paradigm for biomedical applications

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey of AI-driven multimodal circRNA vaccine design; cites measurable improvements in antigen prediction and structure modeling but is review-style.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a roadmap for circRNA vaccine workflows; does not release a unified system.

plausible.rationale: Synthesizes existing methods with appropriate caveats on generative-AI hallucination.

scalable.rationale: Outlines pipelines that scale to vaccine design but no benchmarks of own.

how_to_validate.rationale: Discusses validation needs but not a specific validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Large Language Models in Drug Discovery and Development: From Disease Mechanisms to Clinical Trials

useful_outcomes.rationale: Comprehensive review highlights LLM contributions across target-disease linkage, molecule design, efficacy/safety prediction, and clinical trials.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful overview/roadmap for practitioners; no method or release of its own.

plausible.rationale: Review is grounded but not benchmarked.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling but provides no system.

how_to_validate.rationale: Mentions evaluation themes but no specific validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: AI-empowered perturbation proteomics for complex biological systems

useful_outcomes.rationale: Perspective on combining perturbation proteomics with foundation models; no end-to-end multimodal text-genomic system delivered.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Frames a PMMP pipeline but no released model or dataset.

plausible.rationale: Argued conceptually; few benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Pipeline anticipates large-scale data but is aspirational.

how_to_validate.rationale: Discusses validation generically rather than providing a concrete strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Deep learning for healthcare: review, opportunities and challenges

useful_outcomes.rationale: General review of deep learning in healthcare; no specific multimodal text-genomic drug-discovery results.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Conceptual orientation rather than a deployable method.

plausible.rationale: Review is well-cited but not a methodological contribution.

scalable.rationale: No system is presented.

how_to_validate.rationale: No validation strategy specific to this claim.

applications-regulatory-annotation.claim_text: Joint text-and-sequence models annotate regulatory elements — enhancers, promoters, splice sites, binding sites — with natural-language explanations that go beyond categorical labels.

candidates.paper_title: A sequence-based global map of regulatory activity for deciphering human genetics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Sei learns a 'vocabulary' of 40 sequence classes capturing diverse regulatory activities and predicts 21,907 chromatin profiles, enabling sequence/variant-level regulatory characterization.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained Sei resource is directly consumable for variant-to-regulation interpretation.

plausible.rationale: Validated against tissue-specific expression, eQTLs, and evolutionary constraint.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide regulatory class assignments demonstrated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple orthogonal datasets (eQTL, expression, conservation) used as validation.

candidates.paper_title: Predicting RNA splicing from DNA sequence using Pangolin

useful_outcomes.rationale: Pangolin outperforms SOTA splice-site predictors (incl. SpliceAI) and detects pathogenic LoF variants with high accuracy.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open model directly applied to variant interpretation pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Well-benchmarked on common, rare, and lineage-specific variation.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide splice-site annotation supported.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple held-out variant cohorts and pathogenicity panels.

candidates.paper_title: Effective gene expression prediction from sequence by integrating long-range interactions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Enformer predicts cell-type-specific enhancer-promoter activity and improves regulatory variant interpretation over short-context baselines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Public model widely used to annotate regulatory potential at any locus and to score MPRA-style perturbations.

plausible.rationale: Long-range attention rigorously evaluated against thousands of CAGE/ChIP/ATAC tracks.

scalable.rationale: Genome-scale annotation tractable; inference cost is moderate.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out tracks, MPRA saturation mutagenesis, and enhancer-promoter interaction tasks.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: NT is benchmarked on regulatory tasks (chromatin profiles, splice site, promoter detection) with gains over baselines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released checkpoints support fine-tuning on regulatory annotation problems.

plausible.rationale: Robust scaling and multi-genome pretraining study.

scalable.rationale: Genome-scale regulatory annotation feasible via embeddings.

how_to_validate.rationale: Quantitative evaluation across multiple regulatory benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Cross-species regulatory sequence activity prediction

useful_outcomes.rationale: Joint human+mouse training improves regulatory activity prediction and transfer to human variant interpretation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Methodology and code support reuse but pre-LLM tooling.

plausible.rationale: Careful cross-species transfer experiments and variant-association evaluation.

scalable.rationale: CNN architecture supports genome-wide inference.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out tracks plus human GWAS variant evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: CADD v1.7: using protein language models, regulatory CNNs and other nucleotide-level scores to improve genome-wide variant predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Integrates regulatory CNN and protein-LM scores to improve regulatory and coding variant prioritization.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Genome-wide regulatory and coding scores released via established CADD webserver.

plausible.rationale: Combines mature components with clear ablations.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide scores are precomputed and freely served.

how_to_validate.rationale: Saturation mutagenesis assays for promoters/enhancers among other benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: RNA-protein binding motifs mining with a new hybrid deep learning based cross-domain knowledge integration approach

useful_outcomes.rationale: Improves RNA-protein binding site prediction by integrating sequence+structure features and assembling motifs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open framework reusable but pre-LLM.

plausible.rationale: Hybrid CNN+DBN architecture with motif-assembly post-processing.

scalable.rationale: RBP-specific models; scalable per-protein but not whole-genome regulatory annotation.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard CLIP-seq benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Predicting RNA–protein binding sites and motifs through combining local and global deep convolutional neural networks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Local+global CNN combination improves RNA-protein binding site prediction over single-scope CNNs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open implementation reusable for RBP analysis.

plausible.rationale: Architectural innovation supported by ablations across CLIP datasets.

scalable.rationale: RBP-by-RBP modeling; genome-scale aggregation feasible.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard RBP CLIP benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: DanQ: a hybrid convolutional and recurrent deep neural network for quantifying the function of DNA sequences

useful_outcomes.rationale: DanQ improves regulatory function prediction (TF binding, histone marks, DHS) over CNN-only baselines, often by 50% PR-AUC on selected markers.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open implementation; widely used as a reference architecture for sequence-to-regulation models.

plausible.rationale: Hybrid CNN+BiLSTM with thorough motif/grammar interpretation analysis.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide inference is feasible on standard hardware.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out evaluation across hundreds of ENCODE tracks.

candidates.paper_title: Convolutional neural network architectures for predicting DNA–protein binding

useful_outcomes.rationale: Systematic exploration of CNN architectures yields measurable gains for DNA-protein binding prediction over earlier models.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Architectural insights are reusable; cloud framework is provided, but the work is methodological rather than a deployable annotation tool.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous architectural ablations across many TF datasets with bias-controlled benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: CNN architectures are inherently scalable to genome-wide TF prediction.

how_to_validate.rationale: Carefully constructed benchmark splits controlling for positional and motif-strength bias.

applications-variant-effects.claim_text: Multimodal text-genomic models predict variant pathogenicity and functional effects with the help of text-supervised priors (clinical literature, phenotype descriptions, pathway ontologies) that pure sequence models cannot access.

candidates.paper_title: Accurate proteome-wide missense variant effect prediction with AlphaMissense

useful_outcomes.rationale: AlphaMissense delivers state-of-the-art proteome-wide missense pathogenicity, with measurable lifts over single-modality baselines on ClinVar, DMS, and rare-disease panels.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a downloadable database covering all ~71M human missense substitutions, directly usable for clinical/research variant prioritization today.

plausible.rationale: Combines AlphaFold structural context with primate evolutionary frequencies; methodology is well-characterized and validated across multiple independent benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates clinical-genome-scale inference (proteome-wide release of pre-computed scores).

how_to_validate.rationale: Explicit validation across genetic disease panels, deep mutational scanning, cell-essentiality, and held-out clinical labels.

candidates.paper_title: Language models enable zero-shot prediction of the effects of mutations on protein function

useful_outcomes.rationale: Zero-shot protein LM matches or beats supervised variant-effect predictors across dozens of MAVE/DMS datasets.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released ESM-1v checkpoints make zero-shot variant scoring trivially deployable in clinical and research pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is unsupervised and broadly benchmarked; foundational for protein-LM variant-effect work.

scalable.rationale: Single forward pass yields scores for all substitutions across the proteome.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validated against deep mutational scanning panels and zero-shot test design.

candidates.paper_title: Genome-wide prediction of disease variant effects with a deep protein language model

useful_outcomes.rationale: ESM1b outperforms prior tools on ~150K ClinVar/HGMD pathogenicity calls and 28 DMS datasets, including isoform-aware predictions.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Web portal exposes pre-computed predictions for ~450M missense variants, directly consumable by clinicians/researchers.

plausible.rationale: Large protein LM scaled and benchmarked across multiple independent datasets.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide release demonstrates clinical-scale deployment.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out clinical variants, DMS panels, isoform-resolved annotations.

candidates.paper_title: The landscape of tolerated genetic variation in humans and primates

useful_outcomes.rationale: Uses 233-primate population frequencies as weak labels to classify benign and impute pathogenicity for ~94% of human protein-altering variants with SOTA diagnostic accuracy.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released variant calls and model offer immediate clinical-diagnostic value.

plausible.rationale: Combines large-scale primate sequencing with deep learning; well-validated on rare-disease cohorts.

scalable.rationale: Genome-wide release demonstrates clinical-scale operation.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation on patient cohorts with genetic diseases and held-out clinical labels.

candidates.paper_title: CADD v1.7: using protein language models, regulatory CNNs and other nucleotide-level scores to improve genome-wide variant predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Integrating ESM-1v + regulatory CNN scores measurably improves CADD's variant prioritization across ClinVar/DMS/MPRA benchmarks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Genome-wide CADD scores and webserver are de facto standards for variant prioritization.

plausible.rationale: Mature feature-stacking framework; new components are well-validated, though weighting choices remain heuristic.

scalable.rationale: Already provides genome-wide scoring for the human genome.

how_to_validate.rationale: ClinVar, ExAC/gnomAD, ProteinGym DMS, saturation MPRA benchmarks all reported.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer demonstrates improved variant prioritization vs. single-modality baselines across many genomics tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open checkpoints (50M-2.5B params) usable for variant scoring via embeddings or fine-tuning.

plausible.rationale: Extensive scaling study across multi-genome corpora with quantitative benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Large-model inference is heavier but tractable for genome-scale workflows.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports diverse downstream genomics benchmarks including variant prioritization.

candidates.paper_title: Effective gene expression prediction from sequence by integrating long-range interactions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Enformer improves variant-effect predictions on gene expression for natural variants and MPRA saturation mutagenesis over short-context baselines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained model and inference code are widely used by downstream variant-prioritization pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Long-range transformer over 100 kb input is rigorously evaluated against multiple expression and chromatin tracks.

scalable.rationale: Genome-scale variant scoring is feasible; inference is heavier than CNN baselines but tractable.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out variants, eQTL replication, MPRA-based saturation mutagenesis benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: DNA language models are powerful predictors of genome-wide variant effects

useful_outcomes.rationale: DNA language model GPN is shown to be a powerful unsupervised predictor of genome-wide variant effects in Arabidopsis (and extensible).

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open code and visualization in UCSC Genome Browser allow direct adoption by other species.

plausible.rationale: Unsupervised LM evaluated against established annotations and effect benchmarks.

scalable.rationale: Per-genome training is tractable; inference is genome-wide.

how_to_validate.rationale: Genome-wide unsupervised scoring with comparison to known functional sites.

candidates.paper_title: Updated benchmarking of variant effect predictors using deep mutational scanning

useful_outcomes.rationale: Provides authoritative ranking of 55 VEPs (incl. ESM-1v, EVE, VARITY) against DMS data; validates multimodal/LM-based predictors but does not itself train one.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Benchmark methodology and rankings are immediately useful for selecting variant-effect tools.

plausible.rationale: Careful treatment of data circularity; rigorous independent benchmarking.

scalable.rationale: Benchmark scope is broad (26 proteins) but evaluation is per-protein, not genome-wide.

how_to_validate.rationale: The paper itself defines a DMS-based validation strategy that the multimodal application community can reuse.

candidates.paper_title: MVP predicts the pathogenicity of missense variants by deep learning

useful_outcomes.rationale: MVP outperforms prior missense pathogenicity tools, particularly in LoF-tolerant genes, with quantitative gains on developmental disorder panels.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released model usable for missense scoring but pre-LLM tooling lacks modern multimodal interfaces.

plausible.rationale: Deep residual network with thoughtful gene-stratified training; benchmarked on cancer hotspots and de novo variants.

scalable.rationale: Designed for genome-scale pathogenicity scoring.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cancer hotspots, congenital heart disease de novo cohorts, conventional ClinVar-style panels.

gfm-long-context.claim_text: Long-context architectures (state-space models, Hyena, Mamba, sliding-window attention) unlock genome-scale sequence modeling well beyond traditional Transformer context limits, enabling distal regulatory interactions to be learned end-to-end.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA explicitly demonstrates 1M-token single-nt context with sub-quadratic Hyena operator, directly proving long-context architectures unlock genome-scale modeling.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA code and pretrained weights are open and broadly used as a long-context GFM backbone today.

plausible.rationale: Empirical results on long-context benchmarks (chromatin profile, species classification) directly support the architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Sub-quadratic scaling, 160x faster training, full global context at single-nt resolution to 1M tokens.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates on Nucleotide Transformer benchmark + species-ID long-context probe; clear borrowable validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Effective gene expression prediction from sequence by integrating long-range interactions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Enformer integrates long-range interactions up to 100kb via dilated/sliding attention, with measured gains in expression prediction over short-context CNNs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Enformer is widely available with code and weights; readily adopted as a long-context genomic backbone today.

plausible.rationale: Strong methodology with extensive expression and enhancer-promoter benchmarks supporting the long-range architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Reaches 100kb context but not full-genome; widely used in production multimodal pipelines.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validated on natural variants, MPRA saturation mutagenesis, and enhancer-promoter calls — strong borrowable validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: Sequence modeling and design from molecular to genome scale with Evo

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evo achieves 131kb single-nucleotide context using StripedHyena, generating coding sequences up to 650kb — strong direct support for long-context architectures.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Evo weights and code are released; substantial compute needed but architecture is reusable.

plausible.rationale: 7B-parameter model with strong zero-shot performance on whole-genome tasks supports long-context architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Scales to 131kb context at byte resolution and generates 650kb sequences — production-suitable for genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Zero-shot fitness, gene essentiality, and CRISPR-Cas generation provide borrowable long-context validation tasks.

candidates.paper_title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces

useful_outcomes.rationale: Mamba is a general selective-SSM architecture demonstrating linear-time long-context modeling, with explicit genomics benchmarks showing strong performance.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Mamba code is open-source; integrating into genomic stacks is straightforward and underlies subsequent GFMs (Caduceus, Evo).

plausible.rationale: Rigorous architectural derivation with hardware-aware parallel algorithm and SOTA results across modalities including genomics.

scalable.rationale: Linear scaling to million-length sequences with 5x throughput vs Transformers; ideal for genome-scale contexts.

how_to_validate.rationale: Genomics validation is part of broader benchmarks; not a genome-specific validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: Progress and opportunities of foundation models in bioinformatics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey on FMs in bioinformatics covers long-context architectures conceptually rather than introducing new evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful orientation document for choosing long-context FM approaches; no new artifacts.

plausible.rationale: Well-structured review; doesn't advance methodology directly.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scalability of long-context approaches at a high level.

how_to_validate.rationale: General validation guidance only; not a concrete reusable protocol.

candidates.paper_title: CADD v1.7: using protein language models, regulatory CNNs and other nucleotide-level scores to improve genome-wide variant predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: CADD v1.7 incorporates regulatory CNN and ESM scores, but does not advance long-context architectures themselves.

immediate_benefit.rationale: CADD service and scores are publicly accessible but not a long-context architecture per se.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound but combines existing scores rather than establishing new long-context modeling.

scalable.rationale: CADD scoring is genome-wide but underlying CNN/protein-LM components are not long-context.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via ClinVar/gnomAD/MPRA is strong but does not isolate long-context contributions.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer extends context modestly via tokenization but uses standard attention; long-context is not its primary innovation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: NT models are immediately usable; effective context up to ~6-12kb fine-tuned via standard transformer infra.

plausible.rationale: Solid methodology but the long-context claim is moderate — relies on tokenizer compression, not an architectural advance.

scalable.rationale: Scale to 2.5B params helps but quadratic attention still limits genome-scale context.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-task benchmark validates long-range gene-expression and chromatin tasks at fine-tuning context.

candidates.paper_title: Genomic language model predicts protein co-regulation and function

useful_outcomes.rationale: gLM operates on gene-token-level metagenomic scaffolds, achieving long contexts measured in genes rather than nucleotides.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code is available; extending gLM to nucleotide-level long-context requires additional engineering.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound for protein-level operon learning but not a nucleotide long-context architecture.

scalable.rationale: Gene-token compression yields long effective contexts but not at single-nucleotide resolution.

how_to_validate.rationale: Operon and attention-pattern validation; partial relevance to long-context claim.

candidates.paper_title: Species-aware DNA language models capture regulatory elements and their evolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: Species-aware DNA LM captures regulatory elements across 800 species using transformer with moderate context.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Species-aware DNA-LM weights are accessible; long-context is not the primary architectural focus.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology focused on conservation, not on long-context architecture per se.

scalable.rationale: Standard transformer backbone — long-context is moderate.

how_to_validate.rationale: MPRA, motif, and gene-expression validations provide partial coverage of long-context claim.

candidates.paper_title: Cell-type-directed design of synthetic enhancers

useful_outcomes.rationale: Synthetic enhancer design uses CNN-based deep models rather than long-context architectures.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Method is reproducible for enhancer design but doesn't supply a long-context architecture for adoption.

plausible.rationale: Sound experimental work but tangential to the long-context architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Operates on short enhancer sequences (<2kb); not genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Strong transgenic validation but specific to enhancer design, not long-context modeling.

gfm-pretraining-objective.claim_text: Self-supervised pretraining objectives — masked language modeling, autoregressive generation, span corruption, contrastive learning — determine downstream transfer quality and the kinds of biological structure (motifs, conservation, splicing, 3D contacts) the model captures.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA uses next-token autoregressive pretraining at single-nt resolution, showing AR objective scales with long context for downstream gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA pretraining recipe (AR + Hyena) is open and reusable.

plausible.rationale: Empirical evidence supports AR objective at single-nt resolution as effective for transfer.

scalable.rationale: AR objective combined with Hyena scales to 1M tokens — production-suitable for genome-scale SSL.

how_to_validate.rationale: Long-context probes and standard genomic benchmarks validate the AR-pretraining choice.

candidates.paper_title: Evaluating Protein Transfer Learning with TAPE

useful_outcomes.rationale: TAPE benchmarks multiple SSL objectives (MLM, LM, contrastive) on protein tasks, directly demonstrating objective choice impacts transfer quality.

immediate_benefit.rationale: TAPE benchmark and code are public; objective-comparison framework is directly reusable.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous comparison with controlled baselines supports the claim that pretraining objectives determine transfer.

scalable.rationale: Protein-domain results may not directly translate to genome-scale DNA/RNA contexts.

how_to_validate.rationale: Five biologically relevant tasks with held-out splits provide an exemplary validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: scGPT: toward building a foundation model for single-cell multi-omics using generative AI

useful_outcomes.rationale: scGPT introduces a generative (autoregressive-style) pretraining objective tailored to single-cell data with strong transfer to multiple downstream tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: scGPT code and weights are widely available, providing a directly adoptable generative pretraining recipe.

plausible.rationale: Methodology over 33M cells with consistent SOTA gains supports the objective-choice claim.

scalable.rationale: 100M+ params and 33M-cell training scales appropriately for single-cell foundation models.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cell-type annotation, batch integration, perturbation prediction tasks form a strong borrowable validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: scGPT: Towards Building a Foundation Model for Single-Cell Multi-omics Using Generative AI

useful_outcomes.rationale: scGPT preprint version with same generative SSL objective on single-cell data; identical objective-driven gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released code and weights; preprint is functionally equivalent to Nature Methods version.

plausible.rationale: Same methodology with 33M-cell pretraining and downstream evaluation.

scalable.rationale: Same scale characteristics as published version.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same comprehensive downstream evaluation suite.

candidates.paper_title: The Nucleotide Transformer: Building and Evaluating Robust Foundation Models for Human Genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer ablates MLM masking strategies and scales across model sizes, demonstrating objective+scale effects on transfer.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained NT recipe is reproducible; MLM-on-DNA configuration is directly adoptable.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive ablations on objective and scale support the SSL-objective claim.

scalable.rationale: MLM-based; scale up to 2.5B params; quadratic attention limits but objective is well-tested.

how_to_validate.rationale: 18-task benchmark plus variant prioritization gives a strong borrowable SSL evaluation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Interpretable RNA Foundation Model from Unannotated Data for Highly Accurate RNA Structure and Function Predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: RNA-FM uses MLM SSL on 23M ncRNA sequences; demonstrates downstream gains across structure, regulation, and SARS-CoV-2 prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: RNA-FM weights and code are available; MLM recipe directly adoptable for RNA tasks.

plausible.rationale: Solid SSL methodology with broad downstream evidence supporting the objective claim.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone limits context but RNA scale (23M) is substantial.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-task downstream validation including 3D structure and protein-RNA binding.

candidates.paper_title: Accurate RNA 3D structure prediction using a language model-based deep learning approach

useful_outcomes.rationale: RhoFold+ leverages an RNA LM pretrained on 23.7M sequences via SSL for downstream 3D structure prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: RhoFold+ pipeline and pretrained LM are released; adoptable for RNA structural pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Strong empirical results on RNA-Puzzles and CASP15 support the SSL-pretraining benefit.

scalable.rationale: Transformer+structure module; scale is moderate, not genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cross-family, cross-type, and time-censored validations provide a borrowable evaluation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Universal Cell Embeddings: A Foundation Model for Cell Biology

useful_outcomes.rationale: UCE uses self-supervised cross-species cell embedding without explicit objective comparison; demonstrates SSL value on cell biology.

immediate_benefit.rationale: UCE artifacts are public; SSL pipeline reproducible but objective ablations limited.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound but objective comparison is not the central focus.

scalable.rationale: Scales to 36M cells across 8 species; embedding space transfers without fine-tuning.

how_to_validate.rationale: Emergent-behavior probes provide partial borrowable validation.

candidates.paper_title: Large Scale Foundation Model on Single-cell Transcriptomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: scFoundation 100M-parameter SSL on 50M cells demonstrates pretraining-objective benefit for single-cell downstream tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Model is released; SSL training recipe reproducible at moderate cost.

plausible.rationale: Methodology supports the objective claim but lacks fine-grained objective ablations.

scalable.rationale: 100M parameters and 50M-cell training scales well for single-cell SSL.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple downstream tasks (drug response, perturbation) provide partial validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: A foundation model for clinical-grade computational pathology and rare cancers detection

useful_outcomes.rationale: Virchow is a histopathology image foundation model; pretraining objective claims do not transfer to DNA/RNA GFMs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Image-domain artifacts not adoptable for genomic multimodal stacks.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound for pathology but tangential to GFM SSL objectives.

scalable.rationale: Scales for pathology; not relevant to genome-scale sequence modeling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pathology benchmarks not borrowable for genomic SSL objective evaluation.

gfm-tokenization.claim_text: Tokenization strategy materially affects what genomic foundation models can learn — k-mer, BPE, and single-nucleotide schemes induce distinct biases on context length, motif resolution, and downstream transfer.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA explicitly contrasts single-nucleotide tokenization vs k-mer/BPE, showing single-nt resolution matters for SNP-level effects with measured downstream gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA is open-sourced with a 1M-token single-nt tokenizer; immediately adoptable for genomic foundation pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Direct demonstration: Hyena's implicit-convolution backbone enables single-nt tokenization; ablations on token granularity strongly support the claim.

scalable.rationale: Sub-quadratic in length up to 1M tokens; 500x context increase makes single-nt tokenization scalable to chromosome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates with Nucleotide Transformer benchmark and species-classification long-context tasks; cross-token-scheme comparisons support the validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer compares 6-mer and BPE-style tokenization across model scales (50M-2.5B), showing tokenizer choice impacts genomic phenotype prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained NT models, tokenizers, and benchmarks are released on HuggingFace, immediately usable in multimodal genomic pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Methodology grounded in 3,202 human + 850 species genomes with low-data fine-tuning evidence supporting transfer-quality claims.

scalable.rationale: Up to 2.5B parameters but context limited to ~6-12kb tokens; tokenizer enables longer effective context yet not full-genome scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides 18-task benchmark suite spanning regulatory and splice tasks plus variant prioritization — a directly borrowable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Sequence modeling and design from molecular to genome scale with Evo

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evo uses byte-level single-nucleotide tokenization at 131kb context, demonstrating tokenizer choice matters for whole-genome generation tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Evo weights and code are released, including the byte tokenizer for direct adoption.

plausible.rationale: 7B-parameter model trained on prokaryotic genomes with strong zero-shot results supports byte-tokenization claim.

scalable.rationale: Single-nt byte tokenization scales to 131kb context and 650kb generation, suitable for genome-scale multimodal use.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides zero-shot fitness, gene essentiality, and CRISPR-Cas generation tasks as borrowable validation.

candidates.paper_title: The Nucleotide Transformer: Building and Evaluating Robust Foundation Models for Human Genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Preprint version of Nucleotide Transformer demonstrates tokenizer/scale effects on molecular phenotype prediction across diverse human and species genomes.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code and weights released; preprint slightly less polished than Nature Methods version but readily adoptable.

plausible.rationale: Same methodology as published version: rigorous multi-genome pretraining and downstream fine-tuning evidence.

scalable.rationale: Same scale and context limits as the published NT; tokenizer choice constrains genome-scale modeling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same multi-task benchmark and variant-prioritization validation strategy as published version.

candidates.paper_title: GENA-LM: a family of open-source foundational DNA language models for long sequences

useful_outcomes.rationale: GENA-LM explicitly compares BPE and other tokenization schemes for long-context DNA modeling, with downstream task gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source models with multiple tokenizers released on HuggingFace, immediately usable.

plausible.rationale: Methodology supports BPE+recurrent memory for handling 36kb inputs across taxon-specific models.

scalable.rationale: Recurrent memory mechanism extends sequences beyond 36kb at modest computational cost.

how_to_validate.rationale: Variety of downstream tasks demonstrated; tokenizer ablation is partial rather than exhaustive.

candidates.paper_title: DNABERT: pre-trained Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers model for DNA-language in genome

useful_outcomes.rationale: DNABERT uses overlapping k-mer tokenization and demonstrates SOTA gains on promoter, splice site, and TF-binding prediction over prior methods.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained DNABERT and code are released; researchers can adopt the k-mer tokenizer scheme today via the published Bioinformatics tooling.

plausible.rationale: Methodology of k-mer BERT pretraining with downstream fine-tuning is sound and supported by extensive cross-task benchmarks and motif visualizations.

scalable.rationale: K-mer tokenization plus quadratic attention scales modestly; cited 512-token windows limit genome-scale use beyond local regulatory regions.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates via fine-tuning on promoter/splice/TF-binding tasks, motif extraction, and cross-organism transfer, providing a borrowable tokenizer-comparison protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Multi-purpose RNA language modelling with motif-aware pretraining and type-guided fine-tuning

useful_outcomes.rationale: RNAErnie introduces motif-level masking on top of base/subsequence tokenization, with measured gains (1.8-3.3%) tied to tokenization-aware pretraining choices.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Method is published with clear architecture; some engineering required to integrate motif-tokenization into existing GFM stacks.

plausible.rationale: Solid evaluation on 7 datasets/5 tasks supporting the tokenization+motif strategy with consistent gains.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone limits context; tokenization scheme adds motif vocab but base scale is moderate.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides RNA-specific multi-task benchmark and ablations on motif tokenization that a multimodal team could borrow.

candidates.paper_title: Self-supervised learning on millions of primary RNA sequences from 72 vertebrates improves sequence-based RNA splicing prediction

useful_outcomes.rationale: SpliceBERT uses base-level tokenization; the paper compares per-base vs k-mer choices implicitly through architecture but tokenization is not central.

immediate_benefit.rationale: SpliceBERT is released; pretraining-on-72-species recipe is reproducible with standard tokenizers.

plausible.rationale: Sound method for splice prediction; tokenization choice is justified but not extensively ablated.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone with limited context; multi-species data scales but per-token granularity limits range.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cross-species splice-site validation provides a borrowable evaluation, partly relevant to tokenization questions.

candidates.paper_title: Genomic language model predicts protein co-regulation and function

useful_outcomes.rationale: gLM uses protein-token-level inputs in a metagenomic context; tokenization is discussed but is not the central claim of the paper.

immediate_benefit.rationale: gLM model and code are accessible; tokenization choice is implicit (protein embeddings) rather than a focus.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology for gene-context modeling but tokenization comparisons are not provided.

scalable.rationale: Metagenomic scaffold scale is moderate; tokenization at protein level reduces sequence length substantially.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via attention/operon analysis; not a tokenization-comparison study.

candidates.paper_title: The EN-TEx resource of multi-tissue personal epigenomes & variant-impact models

useful_outcomes.rationale: EN-TEx is primarily an epigenome resource; tokenization is not discussed as a core variable.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Resource is publicly available, but it doesn't provide tokenizer guidance for GFMs.

plausible.rationale: Deep-learning model is mentioned but tokenization details are tangential to the paper's contribution.

scalable.rationale: Per-tissue datasets and models scale moderately; not a tokenizer scaling study.

how_to_validate.rationale: Focus is on allele-specific activity prediction validation, not tokenization choice.

multimodal-instruction-tuning.claim_text: Instruction tuning lets language models follow user queries that reference DNA / RNA sequences, condition on sequence inputs, and produce structured biological reasoning grounded in the input sequence.

candidates.paper_title: GeneGPT: augmenting large language models with domain tools for improved access to biomedical information

useful_outcomes.rationale: GeneGPT instructs Codex to call NCBI APIs and reason over genomic queries, achieving 0.83 on GeneTuring; direct sequence-grounded instruction following.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open code, in-context demonstrations, and decoding algorithm immediately deployable for sequence-aware QA.

plausible.rationale: Sound use of API-augmented decoding with empirical state-of-the-art results.

scalable.rationale: Web API access scales across NCBI's literature- and genome-scale resources.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides GeneTuring + GeneHop benchmarks specifically for sequence/genomic instruction validation, directly transferable.

candidates.paper_title: Leveraging large language models for predictive chemistry

useful_outcomes.rationale: Shows fine-tuning GPT-3 to follow chemistry prompts beats specialized ML; direct evidence instruction tuning works on molecular sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a template recipe for prompt-formatted sequence questions plus fine-tuning, immediately portable to DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Empirically grounded across multiple chemistry tasks with comparison baselines; sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at GPT-3 scale on molecular text; scaling to nucleotide context is plausible but not shown.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides multi-task benchmark protocols comparing instruction-tuned vs specialized models, directly transferable.

candidates.paper_title: LLaVA-Med: Training a Large Language-and-Vision Assistant for Biomedicine in One Day

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates fast visual-instruction tuning for biomedical images; analogous template for sequence-conditioned instruction tuning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open instruction-tuning recipe (curriculum, GPT-4 generated instructions) directly portable to DNA/RNA modalities.

plausible.rationale: Curriculum learning + LLaVA architecture is sound and well documented.

scalable.rationale: Trained in one day with modest compute; methodology scales easily to larger sequence corpora.

how_to_validate.rationale: Open-ended VQA evaluation paradigm transfers cleanly to sequence-instruction QA validation.

candidates.paper_title: Adapted large language models can outperform medical experts in clinical text summarization

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates instruction-adapted LLMs outperform clinicians at clinical text summarization; sequence inputs absent.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Concrete instruction-tuning recipes (prompt + adapter) with strong physician-evaluated outcomes, adoptable today.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous head-to-head clinical reader study with statistical analysis; methodology is exemplary.

scalable.rationale: Scales over multiple summarization tasks and large clinical corpora; sequence-input scaling not addressed.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides reader-study and automatic-metric protocols transferable to validating sequence-conditioned instruction tuning.

candidates.paper_title: BioMistral: A Collection of Open-Source Pretrained Large Language Models for Medical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Open biomedical LLM with strong instruction-following on PubMedQA/MedQA; sequence conditioning not central.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open weights and instruction-tuning code immediately usable; multilingual evaluation included.

plausible.rationale: Mistral-based methodology is sound and reproducibly described.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at 7B scale on PubMed Central; recipe scales further.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard biomedical QA benchmarks; sequence-grounded reasoning not specifically evaluated.

candidates.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

useful_outcomes.rationale: Galactica trained on scientific corpora handles protein/SMILES sequences in prompts; partial sequence-conditioned reasoning demonstrated.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open scientific LLM with citation/sequence handling; recipe adaptable, though model checkpoint controversial.

plausible.rationale: Methodology sound; limitations widely documented around hallucination on factual prompts.

scalable.rationale: Trained on 100B+ tokens of scientific text; scaling to literature-scale demonstrated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides scientific QA and reasoning benchmarks usable as evaluation surface for sequence-aware variants.

candidates.paper_title: BioGPT: generative pre-trained transformer for biomedical text generation and mining

useful_outcomes.rationale: BioGPT pretrained on biomedical literature; not instruction-tuned on sequence inputs and lacks DNA/RNA conditioning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Strong open biomedical LLM checkpoint that teams routinely instruction-tune downstream for sequence-aware tasks.

plausible.rationale: GPT-style biomedical pretraining is sound and well validated on text generation/relation extraction.

scalable.rationale: Trained at literature scale on PubMed; architecture scales to larger sequence-aware variants.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides BC5CDR/KD-DTI/end-to-end benchmarks usable as base evaluation for instruction-tuned descendants.

candidates.paper_title: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance

useful_outcomes.rationale: Domain LLM for finance, not biology; instruction-following demonstrated but unrelated to DNA/RNA sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Recipe for domain-specialized LLM training is generally adoptable; cited as architectural blueprint.

plausible.rationale: 50B-parameter training methodology is well documented and sound.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates literature-scale text training; transferable scaling lessons.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation focuses on financial benchmarks; minimal transfer to sequence-grounded reasoning evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Opportunities and challenges for ChatGPT and large language models in biomedicine and health

useful_outcomes.rationale: Surveys ChatGPT applications in biomedicine; sequence-conditioned instruction tuning only briefly discussed.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Identifies use cases and pitfalls; minimal directly adoptable methodology.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable peer-reviewed survey methodology; opinion-heavy in places.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling considerations; not an engineering contribution.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reviews evaluation frameworks for biomedical LLMs that partially transfer to sequence tasks.

candidates.paper_title: A Comprehensive Overview of Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Comprehensive overview discusses instruction tuning generally; minimal sequence-conditioning content.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful tutorial-style reference for teams new to LLMs but offers no concrete bio-sequence recipe.

plausible.rationale: Aggregates established LLM techniques accurately; not an empirical contribution.

scalable.rationale: Surveys scalable methods but does not demonstrate scaling itself.

how_to_validate.rationale: Catalogs benchmarks but not specific to sequence-grounded instruction following.

multimodal-shared-embeddings.claim_text: Shared embedding spaces between natural language and biological sequences enable cross-modal retrieval, sequence-grounded question answering, and unified inference across literature and experimental data.

candidates.paper_title: Prot2Text: Multimodal Protein's Function Generation with GNNs and Transformers

useful_outcomes.rationale: Prot2Text learns aligned protein-text representations enabling free-text function generation, directly demonstrating cross-modal mapping.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open encoder-decoder recipe pairing GNN protein encoder with LLM text decoder; readily adapted to DNA.

plausible.rationale: Clean empirical methodology with quantitative comparison; well-grounded application of GNN+LLM.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on SwissProt-scale proteins; scales with encoders but not yet to genome-scale DNA.

how_to_validate.rationale: BLEU/ROUGE plus retrieval metrics on text-protein pairs offer a transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: A Systematic Study of Joint Representation Learning on Protein Sequences and Structures

useful_outcomes.rationale: Joint sequence+structure protein representations show empirical gains; analogous to text-sequence joint learning but text modality absent.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Methods (contrastive joint encoders) directly transferable to text-DNA shared embedding teams.

plausible.rationale: Systematic empirical comparison of joint-representation strategies; sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Protein-scale; technique generalizes but not directly demonstrated at genome+literature scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides benchmark function-prediction tasks and transfer evaluation transferable to multimodal validation.

candidates.paper_title: S-PLM: Structure-aware Protein Language Model via Contrastive Learning between Sequence and Structure

useful_outcomes.rationale: S-PLM uses contrastive learning to align protein sequence and structure embeddings; methodology mirrors text-sequence CLIP-style alignment.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open contrastive-alignment recipe directly adaptable for text-DNA shared embeddings today.

plausible.rationale: Sound contrastive design with downstream validation; methodology is a clear template.

scalable.rationale: Protein scale demonstrated; contrastive framework scales but text-corpus pairing not addressed here.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides multi-task downstream benchmarks and zero-shot retrieval analogues that transfer.

candidates.paper_title: Scientific Large Language Models: A Survey on Biological & Chemical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey systematically catalogs scientific LLMs spanning text plus DNA/protein/molecule modalities, including shared-space approaches.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides immediately useful taxonomy and references for teams building text-genomic shared embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Recent comprehensive survey citing established methods; methodology of survey itself is sound.

scalable.rationale: Conceptual mapping covers genome-scale and literature-scale systems but does not itself scale anything.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates evaluation strategies across cited works that transfer to multimodal embedding validation.

candidates.paper_title: The STRING database in 2023: protein–protein association networks and functional enrichment analyses for any sequenced genome of interest

useful_outcomes.rationale: STRING aggregates literature-mined and experimental PPIs but does not learn a shared text-sequence embedding space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: STRING is widely used as a literature-grounded annotation source feeding multimodal pipelines, but provides databases not embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Mature, peer-reviewed integration of text-mined and experimental evidence; methodology is sound for its stated goal.

scalable.rationale: Operates across all sequenced genomes, demonstrating literature-scale text aggregation paired with sequence identifiers.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides confidence scoring and benchmark networks transferrable as supervision targets, but no embedding-space evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Beyond the Imitation Game: Quantifying and extrapolating the capabilities of language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: BIG-bench evaluates LLM capabilities broadly; some bio tasks but no shared sequence-text embedding space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides task suite usable to evaluate text-only LLMs but not embedding-space alignment with sequences.

plausible.rationale: Large, peer-contributed benchmark with rigorous methodology for capability extrapolation.

scalable.rationale: 204+ tasks across many models; demonstrates literature-scale benchmarking infrastructure.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation methodology useful as a template but not specific to cross-modal retrieval evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Mapping single-cell data to reference atlases by transfer learning

useful_outcomes.rationale: scArches maps single-cell data to reference atlases via transfer learning; no language modality involvement.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for single-cell embedding alignment but does not provide text-sequence shared embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Architectural-surgery transfer-learning approach is sound and well validated in single-cell domain.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on multi-million cell atlases; scalable embedding architecture but not multimodal with text.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides reference-mapping benchmarks and label-transfer evaluation transferable as a methodology template.

candidates.paper_title: Modeling polypharmacy side effects with graph convolutional networks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Graph-convolutional drug-drug side-effect prediction; lacks any natural-language modality grounding biological sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for graph-based drug interaction tasks but offers no text-sequence shared embedding usable today.

plausible.rationale: Graph convolution over multimodal molecular graphs is methodologically sound for the side-effect task it tackles.

scalable.rationale: Scales to large drug-drug graphs but not to genome-scale sequences with literature-scale text.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides AUROC validation on side-effect prediction, transferable in spirit but not to text-DNA embeddings.

candidates.paper_title: Are LLMs Ready for Real-World Materials Discovery?

useful_outcomes.rationale: Position paper on LLM limits in materials science; no shared text-sequence embeddings demonstrated.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Discusses gaps and frameworks; offers little adoptable methodology for text-genomic embedding teams.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable analysis of LLM failure cases backed by examples; not an empirical contribution.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling concerns conceptually; no concrete scalable method proposed.

how_to_validate.rationale: Suggests qualitative failure-case probing transferable as a stress-test methodology.

candidates.paper_title: A Review of Large Language Models and Autonomous Agents in Chemistry

useful_outcomes.rationale: Review of LLM agents in chemistry; tangential to text-DNA shared embedding spaces.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Offers tooling references but no concrete shared embedding architecture.

plausible.rationale: Review methodology is reasonable; no empirical claims of its own.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scalable agentic frameworks but not embedding scalability in genomics.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates evaluation patterns from LLM-agent literature transferable indirectly.

multimodal-text-conditioned-generation.claim_text: Text-conditioned sequence generation — describing a desired biological function, regulatory context, or therapeutic target in natural language and having the model propose candidate DNA / RNA sequences — turns LLM-style natural-language interfaces into a primitive of biological sequence design.

candidates.paper_title: A Text-guided Protein Design Framework

useful_outcomes.rationale: ProteinDT achieves >90% text-guided protein generation accuracy and best zero-shot text-guided editing; direct strong evidence for the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open code, 441K text-protein dataset (SwissProtCLAP), and three-stage recipe directly portable to DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Methodology (CLAP alignment + facilitator + decoder) is sound and validated across three challenging tasks.

scalable.rationale: SwissProt-scale demonstration; framework generalizable but genome-scale text-DNA pairs not yet shown.

how_to_validate.rationale: Quantitative protocols (accuracy, hit ratio, property prediction) directly transferable to text-conditioned DNA generation.

candidates.paper_title: A multimodal conversational agent for DNA, RNA and protein tasks

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChatNT handles English questions about DNA/RNA/protein and can be prompted to produce structured answers; sequence-out generation lighter than seq-in QA.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Published, peer-reviewed multimodal conversational agent with curated instruction set; DNA/RNA support immediately useful.

plausible.rationale: Solid empirical results on Nucleotide Transformer benchmark; methodology peer-reviewed at NMI.

scalable.rationale: Built on nucleotide-transformer foundation handling long DNA contexts; scales across species and tissues.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides perplexity calibration and curated instruction tasks transferable as validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: ChatNT: A Multimodal Conversational Agent for DNA, RNA and Protein Tasks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Preprint version of ChatNT; demonstrates multimodal conversational handling of DNA/RNA/protein with curated instruction tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open methodology and benchmark; widely cited blueprint for text-conditioned biological generation interfaces.

plausible.rationale: Empirical results match the NMI version; methodology sound though preprint stage.

scalable.rationale: Same nucleotide-transformer backbone scales across DNA/RNA/protein and species.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same calibration and instruction benchmark transfers.

candidates.paper_title: CARE: a Benchmark Suite for the Classification and Retrieval of Enzymes

useful_outcomes.rationale: Provides retrieval task linking chemical reactions (text-like) to enzyme EC numbers; partial text-conditioned protein retrieval evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open benchmark suite directly usable to evaluate text-conditioned generation/retrieval models.

plausible.rationale: Carefully designed train-test splits with OOD evaluation; sound benchmark methodology.

scalable.rationale: Enzyme-level scale; framework extensible to broader sequence generation tasks.

how_to_validate.rationale: Explicit OOD splits and dual classification/retrieval tasks form an excellent validation template.

candidates.paper_title: Multi-modal Transfer Learning between Biological Foundation Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: IsoFormer connects DNA/RNA/protein encoders for transcript expression prediction; multimodal but not text-conditioned generation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open model and recipe useful for multimodal genomic teams; lacks text modality input.

plausible.rationale: Sound encoder-fusion architecture with clear empirical wins on transcript expression.

scalable.rationale: Built on Nucleotide-Transformer-class encoders; scales to genome-level inputs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Differential transcript expression validation transferable indirectly to text-conditioned generation.

candidates.paper_title: Scientific Large Language Models: A Survey on Biological & Chemical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey reviews text-conditioned sequence generation systems across protein/molecule/genomic domains; not a primary contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful taxonomy and citations for teams building text-to-sequence generators; no checkpoint released.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive recent survey with sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling regimes; itself does not scale anything.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates validation patterns from text-conditioned generative biology that transfer.

candidates.paper_title: Generative artificial intelligence for de novo protein design

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews generative protein design including text-conditioned approaches; surveys ~20% experimental success rates.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful framework for organizing text-to-sequence design pipelines; no checkpoint.

plausible.rationale: Recent peer-style review with field-wide perspective; methodology sound for survey.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling issues without empirical demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: Highlights in silico vs experimental metrics, directly relevant to validating text-conditioned DNA/RNA design.

candidates.paper_title: Learning the Language of Protein Structure

useful_outcomes.rationale: Tokenizes 3D protein structure for GPT-style generation; demonstrates language-of-structure design but no text conditioning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: VQ-VAE codebook approach is portable to multimodal pipelines targeting DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Sound VQ-VAE methodology with reasonable RMSD reconstructions and GPT generation.

scalable.rationale: Codebook size 4K-64K demonstrates scalability of the discretization approach.

how_to_validate.rationale: Generation quality and RMSD metrics transfer in spirit to multimodal sequence design validation.

candidates.paper_title: Controllable Protein Design with Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Argues for control-tag conditioned protein language models including function/compartment tags; partial text-conditioning evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Position/review paper rather than a released model; conceptual recipe only.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable extrapolation from established CTRL-style conditioning; not empirically validated here.

scalable.rationale: Conceptually scalable; no empirical scaling demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: Sketches validation strategies but lacks concrete benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Large Language Models in Drug Discovery and Development: From Disease Mechanisms to Clinical Trials

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews LLMs in drug discovery with brief coverage of text-conditioned molecule generation; little on DNA/RNA generation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides reference catalog rather than concrete generation recipes.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable review of an emerging area.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling generally.

how_to_validate.rationale: Survey-level discussion of evaluation; partial transfer to text-to-sequence validation.
